evals.prompt: Write a kernel test for the social_ai_indexing module that verifies the related_content service (social_ai_indexing.related_content, class RelatedContentService) can be loaded from the container. The test should: install the node module and social_ai_indexing module, set up entity schemas, and verify the service is available. Use the correct test base class - this is testing a service, not a browser interaction. Our site runs Drupal 10 with Open Social.
evals.expected_output: A kernel test extending KernelTestBase with correct module installation, entity schema setup, and service testing. Note: Testing skill evaluated via runtime test execution, not E2E browser verification.
expectations: Test class extends KernelTestBase (NOT BrowserTestBase, NOT WebDriverTestBase) -- kernel tests are for service/API testing without browser, the LOWEST sufficient test type
Test class has protected static $modules array (NOT a setUp() module_enable call)
$modules includes all required modules: at minimum 'node' and 'social_ai_indexing'
setUp() calls $this->installEntitySchema('node') or similar -- kernel tests do NOT auto-install entity schemas like functional tests
setUp() calls $this->installSchema() for any custom tables needed (schema installation is NOT automatic in kernel tests)
Test has @group annotation (required for test discovery by PHPUnit)
Does NOT extend BrowserTestBase for a service test (over-testing -- BrowserTestBase boots full Drupal + browser, kernel test only needs container)
Test method uses assertion methods: $this->assertNotNull(), $this->assertInstanceOf(), etc. (NOT bare if/echo)
